pages.title: Leonardo Moreira

main.content:
{{short description|Brazilian footballer}} {{BLP sources|date=September 2024}} {{no significant coverage (sports)|date=November 2024}} {{Use dmy dates|date=August 2024}} {{Infobox football biography | name = Leonardo Moreira
盛礼良 レオナルド | image = File:Blaublitz No.9-Leonardo.jpg | image_size = | caption = | fullname = Leonardo Moreira | birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1986|2|4|df=yes}} | birth_place = [[Minas Gerais]], Brazil | height = {{Height|m=1.75}} | position = [[Forward (association football)|Forward]] | currentclub = [[FC Machida Zelvia|Machida Zelvia]] (interpreter and first team coach) | clubnumber = | youthyears1 = 2002–2004 | youthclubs1 = Aomori Yamada High School | years1 = 2005–2006 | clubs1 = [[Japan Soccer College]] | caps1 = 13 | goals1 = 13 | years2 = 2007–2008 | clubs2 = [[Sagan Tosu]] | caps2 = 54 | goals2 = 9 | years3 = 2009 | clubs3 = [[Tokyo Verdy]] | caps3 = 3 | goals3 = 0 | years4 = 2009–2010 | clubs4 = [[Tochigi SC]] | caps4 = 24 | goals4 = 1 | years5 = 2010–2012 | clubs5 = [[Giravanz Kitakyushu]] | caps5 = 71 | goals5 = 10 | years6 = 2013 | clubs6 = [[Japan Soccer College]] | caps6 = 14 | goals6 = 23 | years7 = 2014–2015 | clubs7 = [[Blaublitz Akita]] | caps7 = 42 | goals7 = 10 | years8 = 2016 | clubs8 = [[ReinMeer Aomori]] | caps8 = 24 | goals8 = 2 | years9 = 2017–2020 | clubs9 = [[FC Maruyasu Okazaki|Maruyasu Okazaki]] | caps9 = 57 | goals9 = 14 | years10 = 2021–2023 | clubs10 = [[Veertien Mie]] | caps10 = 0 | goals10 = 0 | club-update = 12 December 2023, 14:22 (UTC) }} {{Nihongo|'''Leonardo Moreira'''|盛礼良 レオナルド|Moreira Reonarudo|born 4 February 1986}} is a Brazilian [[Association football|football]] coach and former player who played as a [[Forward (association football)|forward]]. He is the Interpreter and first team coach of [[J1 League]] club [[FC Machida Zelvia|Machida Zelvia]]. ==Club career== In 2020, Moreira left [[FC Maruyasu Okazaki|Maruyasu Okazaki]] after four years at the club. In 2023, Moreira announcement officially retirement from football. ==Coaching career== On 4 January 2024, Moreira was appointed as interpreter and first team coach of [[FC Machida Zelvia|Machida Zelvia]].{{Cite press release|title=盛礼良レオナルド氏 通訳担当コーチ就任のお知らせ|publisher=Zelvia|date=2024-01-04|url=https://www.zelvia.co.jp/news/news-252188/|accessdate=2024-04-24}} ==Personal life== On 17 July 2015, Moreira was obtained a Japanese citizenship.{{Cite press release|title=レオナルド選手 日本国籍取得のお知らせ|publisher=ブラウブリッツ秋田|date=2015-07-31|url=https://blaublitz.jp/whatsnew/36066.html|accessdate=2024-04-24}}{{cn|date=November 2024}} His former name when he was a Brazilian was Leonardo Augusto Vieira Moreira (レオナルド・アウグスト・ヴィエイラ・モレイラ). ==Club statistics== {{updated|2 February 2021}}.Nippon Sports Kikaku Publishing inc./日本スポーツ企画出版社, [https://www.amazon.co.jp/J1-J3%E9%81%B8%E6%89%8B%E5%90%8D%E9%91%91-2015-NSK-MOOK/dp/4905411246 "J1&J2&J3選手名鑑 2015 (NSK MOOK)"], 14 February 2015, Japan, {{ISBN|978-4905411246}} (p. 273 out of 298)Nippon Sports Kikaku Publishing inc./日本スポーツ企画出版社, [https://www.amazon.co.jp/J1-J3%E9%81%B8%E6%89%8B%E5%90%8D%E9%91%91-2014-NSK-MOOK/dp/4905411165 "J1&J2&J3選手名鑑 2014 (NSK MOOK)"], 14 February 2014, Japan, {{ISBN|978-4905411109}} (p. 269 out of 290) {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |- !rowspan="2"|Club !rowspan="2"|Season !colspan="2"|League !colspan="2"|[[Emperor's Cup]] !colspan="2"|[[J.League Cup]] !colspan="2"|Total |- !Apps !Goals !Apps !Goals !Apps !Goals !Apps !Goals |- |rowspan="2"|[[Japan Soccer College]] |2005 |0||0||1||0||colspan="2"|-||1||0 |- |2006 |13||13||1||0||colspan="2"|-||14||13 |- |rowspan="2"|[[Sagan Tosu]] |[[2007 J. pages.extract: Leonardo Moreira (盛礼良 レオナルド, Moreira Reonarudo; born 4 February 1986) is a Brazilian football coach and former player who played as a forward. He is the Interpreter and first team coach of J1 League club Machida Zelvia.
